canonical_question: How do you integrate ERP with tax engines like Avalara, Vertex, or Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE?
aliases: ERP tax engine integration Avalara Vertex ONESOURCE
How to connect SAP or Oracle ERP to an external tax calculation engine
Real-time tax determination integration architecture for ERP systems
Tax code mapping and exemption certificate management in ERP integrations

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Vertex REST API v1 EOL April 30, 2026; Avalara MCP support added 2025

constraints: Avalara AvaTax rate limits enforced per account — HTTP 429 returned when exceeded; exact thresholds vary by plan
Vertex O Series REST API v1 end-of-life April 30, 2026 — must migrate to v2
Tax engine latency must stay under 200ms per call for real-time order entry
Exemption certificates must be validated and stored in the tax engine before exempt transactions process
Tax code mapping mandatory — unmapped items default to fully taxable
Multi-currency transactions require exchange rate conversion before sending to tax engine
Filing integration is separate from tax calculation in all three engines

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need ERP-to-ERP integration without tax calculation
Need e-commerce tax only without ERP
Need VAT/GST compliance reporting only, not real-time calculation
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Consult tax engine vendor compliance module docs

related_to.label: AP Automation Playbook — use tax on purchases
Revenue Recognition — tax impacts on rev rec

solves.label: O2C playbook that needs tax calculation step

inputs_needed.question: Which ERP system are you integrating?
options: Oracle ERP Cloud or E-Business Suite

inputs_needed.question: Which tax engine are you evaluating or implementing?
What transaction types need tax calculation?
